channel.title: Military on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Military on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Rendlesham Forest incident
item.description:
The Rendlesham Forest incident involved reported sightings of unexplained lights near Rendlesham Forest in Suffolk, England, in December 1980. USAF personnel claimed to have seen a UFO, and the event is often called “Britain’s Roswell.” Sceptics suggest the sightings were misinterpretations of a fireball, the Orfordness Lighthouse, and bright stars. The UK Ministry of Defence stated the event posed no threat to national security and was not investigated as such.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rendlesham_Forest_incident

item.title: National Investigations Committee On Aerial Phenomena
item.description:
The National Investigations Committee On Aerial Phenomena (NICAP) was a UFO research organization active in the United States from 1956 to 1980. Despite facing financial difficulties, NICAP gained significant visibility and respectability, advocating for scientific investigation of UFO sightings and arguing for a governmental cover-up of UFO evidence. The organization’s membership fluctuated, peaking in the early 1960s before declining sharply after the publication of the Condon Report. NICAP officially dissolved in 1980, and its archives were later acquired by the Center for UFO Studies.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Investigations_Committee_On_Aerial_Phenomena

item.title: Pentagon Reopens Search for Alleged ‘Yankee Blue’ Memo After Initial Denial
item.description:
A FOIA appellate decision has ordered the Department of War to conduct additional searches for a 2023 memorandum. This memorandum was reportedly issued by the Secretary of Defense’s office to halt an Air Force hazing ritual called “Yankee Blue,” which involved fabricated claims of reverse-engineering non-human technology. Initially, the Department of War stated no such memorandum was found, but the appeal has now been granted, requiring a new search. The original Wall Street Journal article described the alleged directive but did not publish the memo or provide specific sourcing.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/pentagon-reopens-search-for-alleged-yankee-blue-memo-after-initial-denial

item.title: UFO Reports and Atomic Sites
item.description:
Since 1947, US media has speculated about a link between UFO reports and atomic sites. Early in the Cold War, domestic UFO reports were taken seriously, with some media suggesting they were related to atomic energy transmutation, a claim later dismissed. By the 1990s, legends arose of UFOs interfering with missile tests and nuclear complexes. Statistical studies suggest UFO reports are more common near military or atomic sites, though experts argue this may be due to increased scrutiny of these areas.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_reports_and_atomic_sites

item.title: Green fireballs
item.description:
Green fireballs are a type of unidentified flying object reported since the early 1950s, primarily in the southwestern United States. Early reports from late 1948 described them as bright green balls of fire or like huge green meteors. While some ufologists suggest an extraterrestrial origin, mainstream explanations include natural bolides. Investigations in the late 1940s and early 1950s, including Project Twinkle, were unable to definitively identify the origin, with official conclusions leaning towards natural phenomena.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Green_fireballs
